rounds.prompt: How many times wider is a grain of sand than a human hair?
rounds.fact: A human hair is ~70 µm wide; a grain of sand is ~1,000 µm (1 mm) — about 14× wider.

rounds.prompt: How many times larger is a human cell than a bacterium?
rounds.fact: A bacterium is ~1–2 µm; a typical human cell is ~10–20 µm — about 10× larger. But your body has roughly equal numbers of both.

rounds.prompt: How many times larger is a bacterium than a virus?
rounds.fact: A flu virus is ~100 nanometres; a bacterium is ~1–10 micrometres — roughly 10–100× larger. Viruses can infect bacteria.

rounds.prompt: How many times wider is a human hair than a typical virus?
rounds.fact: A flu virus is ~100 nanometres; a human hair is ~70,000 nanometres — 700× wider. A single hair could accommodate 700 flu viruses side by side.

rounds.prompt: How many times larger is an atom than the nucleus inside it?
rounds.fact: An atomic nucleus is ~1 femtometre; the full atom is ~100,000 femtometres — 100,000× larger. If the nucleus were a marble, the atom would be a football stadium.
